result.markdown:
--- title: "Linear model" subtitle: "Fit voxel-wise GLMs from a design matrix." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Build a Design ::: {#0f6da33a .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": np.arange(0, 96, 8), "condition": ["face", "scene", "tool"] * 4, }) model = fm.event_model( 'onset ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")', data=events, sampling_frame=fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=120, TR=1.0), ) X = np.asarray(model.design_matrix) X.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` (120, 3) ``` ::: ::: ## Simulate Voxel Data ::: {#b2d39ac3 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} rng = np.random.default_rng(5) n_voxels = 6 true_beta = np.array([ np.linspace(0.8, 1.2, n_voxels), np.linspace(-0.4, 0.2, n_voxels), np.linspace(0.1, 0.5, n_voxels), ]) Y = X @ true_beta + rng.normal(scale=0.08, size=(X.shape[0], n_voxels)) Y.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` (120, 6) ``` ::: ::: ## Fit OLS `fmri_ols_fit()` is a compact matrix-level helper. Rows are time points and columns are voxels. ::: {#29b1edc8 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit = fm.fmri_ols_fit(Y, X) fit["beta"].shape, fit["se"].shape, fit["t"].shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` ((3, 6), (3, 6), (3, 6)) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#53814db5 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} beta = pd.DataFrame(fit["beta"], index=model.column_names) beta.round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4 5
condition_condition.face 0.774 0.883 0.972 1.026 1.117 1.209
condition_condition.scene -0.390 -0.297 -0.170 -0.040 0.085 0.170
condition_condition.tool 0.084 0.188 0.275 0.353 0.408 0.512
``` ::: ::: ## Compute a Contrast ::: {#3fd5021d .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} columns = model.column_names face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(columns)) face_vs_scene[columns.index("condition_condition.face")] = 1 face_vs_scene[columns.index("condition_condition.scene")] = -1 contrast_beta = face_vs_scene @ fit["beta"] contrast_beta.round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` array([1.163, 1.18 , 1.143, 1.066, 1.032, 1.039]) ``` ::: ::: ## Voxel-Wise Covariates The compatibility helper can append one voxel-specific nuisance column per voxel. ::: {#69db1090 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} voxelwise_motion = rng.normal(size=Y.shape) fit_with_voxel_cov = fm.fmri_ols_fit( Y, X, voxelwise=voxelwise_motion, voxel_name="motion_proxy", ) fit_with_voxel_cov["beta"].tail(1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ```{=html}
0 1 2 3 4 5
motion_proxy -0.006987 -0.001733 0.001157 0.005465 -0.014795 -0.011865
``` ::: ::: ## Next - [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd) - [Sketched and AR-aware models](sketched-ar-models.qmd)
